title: eryngium amethystinum var. tenuifolium
intro: Eryngium amethystinum var. tenuifolium, also known as the Sea Holly or Blue Eryngium, is a variety of flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Eryngium amethystinum var. tenuifolium is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and its leaves and roots can be used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
